steps.title: Set up the distance equation
steps.content:
For each part of the trip, distance equals rate times time. The first $x$ hours at 60 mph covers $60x$ miles. The remaining $y$ hours at 50 mph covers $50y$ miles. The total distance is 210 miles, so:
$$60x + 50y = 210$$

steps.title: Substitute x = 1 and solve for y
steps.content:
We know $x = 1$, so substitute into the equation:
$$60(1) + 50y = 210$$
$$60 + 50y = 210$$
Subtract 60 from both sides:
$$50y = 150$$
Divide both sides by 50:
$$y = 3$$
